other_observers: Riley Morris, Leland Flores, Susan Allerton; I am the original finder of this bird. Additional observers independent from me were; Jim Beatty, Nancy Fisher, John & Jan Reese, Rich Donaly, Amy Dobbins
specific_location: Pastorius State Wildlife Area. East side of lake near the outhouse. West side of the road under the oakbrush and junipers.
description: Small bird (warbler sized) with two dark stripes on the crown of the head with rusty colored stripe in between these two stripes. Pink colored bill. Dark spots on sides of upper breast, rather large in size.Behaviors: Bird was very skittish/shy. Moved away quickly when spooked by cars/people. Stayed mostly on the ground hopping along. Didn't scratch at leaves on ground like a thrush. Very quite in underbrush unlike a thrush.Call: Not singing/callingPlumage: Unknown
